data.body:
## Decision record — Revision 1 (2026-06-17, post Opus review)

Amends the "Execution decisions" comment after an adversarial code-grounded review.
Verdict was needs-revision (NOT a Jira-sync risk — reconciler confirmed safe). Five
corrections; the first changes an earlier decision, the rest pin under-specified seams.

**R1.1 — CRITICAL fix to Decision #1: derive `updated_at` in `public_state`, NOT on
the reduced dict.** Earlier wording ("surfaced on the state dict") was unsafe:
`compact.py:93,124` serializes the exact dict `reduce_ticket()` returns verbatim into
the SNAPSHOT event's `compiled_state`, and `process_snapshot` (`_processors.py:367`)
copies every key back on replay. Putting `updated_at` on the reduced dict would write a
derived field into event-log bytes -> breaks the no-event-schema / I3-clean / read-only
claim and trips canonical-bytes parity tests. CORRECTED DECISION: compute `updated_at`
in `public_state` (`reducer/_present.py`) — applied at every interface output site
(show/list/search/ready), NOT in the compaction path — as
`max(created_at, latest replayed event ts, snapshot ts)`.
New AC: a compacted ticket's SNAPSHOT event bytes are byte-identical with and without
this change (no `updated_at` key in `compiled_state`).

**R1.2 — SNAPSHOT-seed for the timestamp max (correctness).** `replay_events` skips all
pre-SNAPSHOT events (`_processors.py:430`), so a running-max taken only inside the
replay loop computes `updated_at` from an empty post-snapshot set for a freshly-compacted
ticket. Deriving in `public_state` as `max(created_at, last-event-ts, snapshot
compacted_at/ts)` sidesteps the replay-skip. The max must include the SNAPSHOT event's
own `compacted_at`/`timestamp`.

**R1.3 — None-last sort technique (correctness).** Implement the sort key as a
`(value_is_none, value)` tuple (or explicit sentinel), NOT `key or 0`. `key or 0` sorts
an unset `priority` as priority-0 (highest), not last, and mixed None/int otherwise
raises TypeError in Py3. Unset values sort LAST in both directions consistently.

**R1.4 — Resolve the `OR`-keyword vs comma-OR contradiction.** DECISION: comma-OR
within a field is the ONLY OR mechanism in v1 (`status:open,in_progress`); there is NO
bare `OR` keyword. The epic body's success-criterion line `search "... OR ..."` is
SUPERSEDED — update it to the comma form `search "status:open,in_progress"`. A literal
`OR` token is treated as free-text substring (degrade rule), not a boolean operator.
Bare-`OR`/parenthetical cross-field boolean is the explicitly-deferred future item.

**R1.5 — State the current default order + reconcile the AC line.** Current default
order returned by `reduce_all_tickets` is ticket-id directory order
(`sorted(os.listdir(...))`, `_api.py`). `--sort` applies a stable sort on top of this;
absent `--sort`, output is byte-identical to today (goldens hold). Reconcile the epic AC
checkbox "`--sort` on list/search/ready (CLI)" with Decision #6: predicates are
search-ONLY; `--sort` is available on list/search/ready.
